title: Governance Record Correction — Decision ID Mapping
summary: Publicly acknowledge and document that 9 of 11 on-chain decision records contain local decision files with incorrect decisionId values. The on-chain hashes and URIs remain valid — the error is confined to the decisionId field within the JSON files themselves. This proposal records the canonical mapping on-chain and documents the root cause fix applied to evaluate.py.
rationale: The Algorithmic Manager (evaluate.py) previously generated decisionId values without querying on-chain state. The AM would hallucinate or reuse IDs (e.g., 5001, 6001, 1) because it had no knowledge of the contract's sequential counter. Since on-chain records are immutable (no amend function in OpenInnovateGovernanceV2) and modifying local files would break hash integrity, the only governance-aligned fix is transparent acknowledgment. Per our Root Thesis Maxim: governance systems must serve their participants through honesty, not convenience.
rootCause: evaluate.py delegated decisionId assignment to the AM system prompt without providing on-chain state. The AM generated plausible but incorrect values. The error compounded because on-chain recording happened after file creation, and no reconciliation step existed.

correctionActions.description: Created governance/DECISION-ID-MAP.md with canonical on-chain-to-file mapping
Added get_next_decision_id() to evaluate.py — queries on-chain decisionCount() and overwrites AM-generated decisionId before file write
Submit this proposal through the governance pipeline and record on-chain, establishing a permanent transparent acknowledgment of the error and its resolution

constraints: On-chain decision records are immutable — no update or amend function exists in the contract
Local decision files cannot be modified without breaking their on-chain hash integrity
Execution records already use correct on-chain IDs (created after on-chain recording)
The on-chain URI field remains the canonical link between files and chain records

milestones.description: DECISION-ID-MAP.md created with canonical mapping for all 11 decisions
evaluate.py patched with get_next_decision_id() to query on-chain state before writing decisionId
Correction proposal evaluated by AM and decision recorded on-chain as transparent public acknowledgment
